# Estela

> Multi-Agent AI Coding Assistant - Desktop app built with Tauri + SolidJS

## Quick Start

- `npm run tauri dev` - Development mode
- `npx tsc --noEmit` - Type check
- API keys: Settings modal in app

## Architecture Overview

```
src/
├── components/     # UI components (SolidJS)
├── config/         # Constants, env validation
├── hooks/          # React-style hooks (useChat)
├── services/       # Business logic
│   ├── auth/       # Credentials, OAuth
│   ├── llm/        # LLM clients (streaming)
│   ├── database.ts # SQLite operations
│   └── migrations.ts
├── stores/         # Global state (SolidJS signals)
└── types/          # TypeScript types
```

## Key Files

### Entry Points
- src/App.tsx - Root component with initialization
- src/index.tsx - SolidJS render entry

### State Management
- src/stores/session.ts - Session, messages, agents state
- src/hooks/useChat.ts - Chat streaming logic

### Services
- src/services/database.ts - SQLite CRUD operations
- src/services/llm/client.ts - LLM provider abstraction
- src/services/auth/credentials.ts - API key storage

### Components
- src/components/chat/ - Chat UI (MessageList, MessageInput)
- src/components/sessions/ - Session management UI
- src/components/settings/ - Settings modal

## Data Flow

```
User Input → useChat.sendMessage()
           → saveMessage() to SQLite
           → resolveAuth() picks provider
           → createClient() streams response
           → session.updateMessageContent() updates UI
           → updateMessage() saves final response
```

## Provider Priority

1. OAuth token (if available)
2. Direct API key (Anthropic, OpenAI)
3. OpenRouter gateway (fallback)

## Database Schema

- sessions: id, name, created_at, updated_at, status
- messages: id, session_id, role, content, tokens_used
- agents: id, session_id, type, status, model
- file_changes: id, session_id, agent_id, file_path, change_type
